description:
A container-platform specialist recently added a small CSV inventory of micro-services and now wants a machine-readable port map that other automation can consume.

  Your objective
  1. Read the CSV file /home/user/microservices/services.csv (already present).
  2. Create a new directory  /home/user/microservices/maps   if it does not yet exist.
  3. Transform the CSV into a single, compact JSON object that maps each service name to the numeric port it exposes.
     • The JSON file MUST be written to  /home/user/microservices/maps/port-map.json
     • It MUST be formatted exactly like the example below—one opening brace, one key/value pair per line, a trailing newline after the closing brace, and no additional whitespace before or after the brace pair.  
       Example of the exact required format (do not sort, keep the services in the order they appear in the CSV):

{
  "auth-service": 8080,
  "payment-service": 9090,
  "frontend": 80
}

  4. Create a very short transformation log so CI can confirm the step ran:  
     • The log file MUST be /home/user/microservices/transform.log  
     • The file MUST contain exactly the single word SUCCESS followed by a single newline character, nothing else.

  Hints
  • Only 2–3 shell commands should be sufficient (think cut/awk + jq, or similar).  
  • You do NOT need sudo for any part of this task.

  The automated grader will confirm:
  • /home/user/microservices/maps/port-map.json exists and its content matches the required JSON byte-for-byte (including the final newline).  
  • /home/user/microservices/transform.log exists and contains exactly “SUCCESS\n”.  
  • No additional output files were created inside /home/user/microservices/maps.

  Finish when both files meet the specifications.
truth:
Initial filesystem state provided to the agent:
  1. Directory /home/user/microservices already exists with 0755 permissions.
  2. File /home/user/microservices/services.csv exists with 0644 permissions and the following exact content (including the final newline):

service_name,image,port
auth-service,ghcr.io/org/auth:1.0,8080
payment-service,ghcr.io/org/pay:1.0,9090
frontend,ghcr.io/org/front:2.1,80

  Expected artifacts after the agent finishes:

  A. /home/user/microservices/maps/port-map.json  (0644) with the exact 63-byte content shown below, including the trailing newline:

{
  "auth-service": 8080,
  "payment-service": 9090,
  "frontend": 80
}

  B. /home/user/microservices/transform.log  (0644) containing exactly 8 bytes:

SUCCESS\n
name: task_000000_c0c45e94
